purpose: needle-in-haystack probe: does the runtime actually process the full submitted prompt? Audits the July 8-9 calibrated long-context cells whose 4k and 8k TTFT were identical (~2.83s) across both days.

method.needle: unique fact 'the secret budget code for this project is ZEBRA-7741' planted at the transcript start or end
method.question: what is the secret budget code mentioned in the transcript? Answer with just the code.

conclusion.finding: keep-last input truncation: content at the start of the prompt is unrecoverable from roughly 3.3k submitted tokens onward, while usage.prompt_tokens still reports the full submitted count (e.g. 7583).
invalidates: July 8-9 calibrated long-context 'pass' cells for this model (uniform-filler transcript was truncation-blind)
the '8k TTFT 2.831s vs FLM 22.697s' comparison (compared ~2-3k of real prefill against FLM's honest 8k)
the qwen25_longctx_quality and qwen25_longctx_sizes clauses of the July 9 replace-FLM gate
conclusion.unaffected: short-task grammar (40/40) and prompt (45/50) results; prompts fit well inside the effective window

side_findings: NPU exclusivity: with `flm serve` running, every Lemonade inference failed HTTP 500 (RyzenAI DynamicDispatch 'Failed to submit'); the backend stayed wedged after FLM was stopped and recovered only after model unload+reload.
TTFT non-monotonicity corroborates truncation: 3.3k tokens cost 4.06s TTFT but 7.6k tokens cost 3.21s.
harness_fix: tools/provider_bench.py long_context_prompt now plants LONGCTX_OPEN_CODE/LONGCTX_CLOSE_CODE at both transcript ends and check_longctx_contract requires both to be quoted (start/end needle flags + truncation_suspected), unit-tested in tests/test_provider_bench.py.
